product_overview: Adhera is a web-based medication adherence platform that helps patients track doses, receive reminders, and report side effects. It also gives healthcare providers and administrators visibility into adherence, patient assignments, and account status.
core_goals: Enable patients to register and authenticate securely
Provide health and database status visibility via a health check endpoint
Allow providers to view pending patient requests and assigned patient adherence data
Support secure provider login and authenticated access to provider resources
Lay the foundation for medication adherence tracking, dashboards, and side-effect reporting as described in the PRD

features.name: Health Check API
features.description: Provides a lightweight endpoint to verify that the API service is running and the database connection is healthy.
user_flows: GET /v1/health -> Receive 200 SuccessResponse confirming API and database status
GET /v1/health when the database is unavailable -> Receive non-200 error response indicating service health check failure

features.name: Authentication API
features.description: Supports user registration and login for patients and healthcare providers using the backend authentication service.
user_flows: POST /v1/auth/signup with valid patient or provider registration data -> Receive 200 SuccessResponse indicating account creation
POST /v1/auth/login with valid email and password -> Receive 200 SuccessResponse with authenticated session details
POST /v1/auth/signup with missing or invalid required fields -> Receive validation error response and account is not created
POST /v1/auth/login with invalid credentials -> Receive authentication error response and no session is issued

features.name: Provider API
features.description: Allows authenticated providers to access provider-specific work queues such as pending requests.
user_flows: POST /v1/auth/login with valid provider credentials -> Receive 200 SuccessResponse and auth token/session -> GET /v1/provider/pending-requests with valid token -> Receive 200 SuccessResponse containing pending provider requests
GET /v1/provider/pending-requests without authentication -> Receive authorization error response and no request data is returned
GET /v1/provider/pending-requests with an expired or invalid token -> Receive 401 or 403 error response and access is denied

features.name: Authentication API
features.description: Handles signup, login, password resets, and forgot password endpoints

features.name: Provider API
features.description: Handles pending requests, assignments, and dashboards
